paper_metadata.title: One Embedder, Any Task: Instruction-Finetuned Text Embeddings
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce INSTRUCTOR, a new method for computing text embeddings given task instructions: every text input is embedded together with instructions explaining the use case (e.g., task and domain descriptions). Unlike encoders from prior work that are more specialized, INSTRUCTOR is a single embedder that can generate text embeddings tailored to different downstream tasks and domains, without any further training. We first annotate instructions for 330 diverse tasks and train INSTRUCTOR on this multitask mixture with a contrastive loss. We evaluate INSTRUCTOR on 70 embedding evaluation tasks (66 of which are unseen during training), ranging from classification and information retrieval to semantic textual similarity and text generation evaluation. INSTRUCTOR, while having an order of magnitude fewer parameters than the previous best model, achieves state-of-the-art performance, with an average improvement of 3.4% compared to the previous best results on the 70 diverse datasets. Our analysis suggests that INSTRUCTOR is robust to changes in instructions, and that instruction finetuning mitigates the challenge of training a single model on diverse datasets. Our model, code, and data are available at https://instructor-embedding.github.io.

extraction_data.innovation: INSTRUCTOR introduces a unified instruction-finetuned text embedding model that uses a single encoder architecture to support diverse downstream tasks via task-specific instructions. It leverages a bidirectional in-batch sampled loss with positive and hard negative pairs, trained on a new multitask dataset (MEDI) with 330 instruction-based embedding tasks.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: MEDI (330 diverse text embedding datasets with human-written task instructions)
extraction_data.extension: Uses a unified instruction template with structured components (TextType, TaskObjective, Domain) to enable consistent, task-specific embedding generation across diverse domains and tasks.
extraction_data.research_problem: General-purpose text embeddings that perform well across diverse tasks and domains without task-specific fine-tuning
extraction_data.application: Text classification, information retrieval, semantic textual similarity, question answering, fact checking, prompt retrieval, clustering, sentiment analysis, document reranking, pair classification
extraction_data.paper_title: One Embedder, Any Task: Instruction-Finetuned Text Embeddings

extraction_data.innovation: GTR-Base is a base-sized model pretrained on a diverse corpus for general-purpose text embedding, serving as a baseline for instruction-finetuning in the INSTRUCTOR framework.
extraction_data.research_problem: General-purpose text embeddings with strong zero-shot transferability across diverse tasks and domains
extraction_data.application: Information retrieval, text similarity, classification, clustering, prompt retrieval for few-shot in-context learning
extraction_data.paper_title: One Embedder, Any Task: Instruction-Finetuned Text Embeddings
